full_text:
The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase, followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H) by Sarah.

Dispase dissociation media included 38.8 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 800 µl Dispase + 400 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C rotating for 30 min, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 35.6 ml DMEM/F12+++, 4 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 400 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C, rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

Dispase dissociated cells:

(1) First RBC lysis with 3 ml for 5 min + 12 ml DMEM/F12+++. (1) Second RBC lysis with 1 ml for 5 min + 6 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was resuspended in 10 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media.

Sequentially dissociated cells: (1) RBC lysis with 3 ml for 5 min + 12 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was resuspended in 5 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media.

Cell counts:

B) Biobanking

Dispase dissociated cells: 6x 10x10^6 cells

Cells were frozen in 1mL of STEM-CellBanker.

C) Experiment

Fresh cells were used for ADC titration experiment, to determine (sub)optimal concentrations of ADCs that produce measurable biological effects without causing excessive cell death. These concentrations will be used in downstream single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) experiments.

→See 15_Log_file_wet_lab on Google drive
